phase3_findings.description: N1 (shadow-promoted, corroborated x2): 'Both local-diff producers check the raw diff, filtered candidate, promotion write, and stdout publication' falsely attributes Phase 0.2's cache-producer pipeline to the item-6a pre-check (which is pipefail + rc routing only). In .changeset/issue-503-diff-base-fix.md:6 and docs/DEVFLOW_SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md:371.

N2 (shadow-promoted, corroborated x3): item-6a PR-mode arm (exit-65 guard + REVIEW_DIFF_BASE=$HEAD_OVERRIDE_BASE) never executed, pinned deletion-only. Added behavioral execution tests (operand carried -> honored; dropped -> exit 65); mutation-verified both catch guard defeat.
#503 assertion label said 'stages the filtered diff' but pins the raw-candidate (pre-awk) stage.

rows: UNRESOLVABLE	R3	.changeset/issue-503-diff-base-fix.md	6	count-locked: '3 item' claim but no adjacent assertion block (informational, never gates)

skipped_checks.reason: working tree dirty at the pre-commit suite run (grades committed HEAD); item-6a stale-prose pre-check ran clean on committed HEAD (rc 0), and the committed-tree confirming run clears the skip

phase3_findings.description: F1 (corroborated x3: silent-failure-hunter + pr-test-analyzer + requesting-code-review): item-6a fence discriminates PR-vs-current-branch mode on `test -n ${PR_BASE_BRANCH:-}`, but the prose only instructs carrying $HEAD_OVERRIDE_BASE, not the PR_BASE_BRANCH discriminator. If the agent carries the operand but not the mode-signal, the fence silently falls to the current-branch arm (origin/$BASE), bypassing the exit-65 guard — the base-misattribution class #503 exists to close, via a different door. Fails open where it claims to fail closed.

The #363 head-count assertion label carries a stale copy-paste clause ('the comment fetch still writes via an in-workspace redirect, NOT a tee, so gh exit status survives') that misdescribes the #503 producer (no comment fetch / gh in that fence).

F2 (known/accepted, from iter-1): config-get hard-failure defaults to origin/main in a read-only review rather than failing closed like update-branch-checkpoint.sh. Prose now accurately describes this (comment-analyzer confirmed); accepted tradeoff for the advisory read-only surface.
